chunks.text: February 28, 2019: 2019 EECS WHM Events. WHM Kickoff Event - Succulents and Breakfast 3/1; Research Talks 3/7; JewelrEE Making Social 3/7; Movie Screening - Hidden Figures 3/8; Ice Cream Social 3/13; Lunch and Learn 3/19, Speaker Panel - Women in EECS 3/20.
chunks.question: What events are listed for the 2019 EECS WHM?
chunks.answer: Kickoff, Research Talks, JewelrEE Social, Movie, Ice Cream, Lunch, Speaker Panel.

chunks.text: February 26, 2019: Berkeley EECS Women’s History Month (WHM) Kickoff Event. Come join Google, the Berkeley EECS Department, and the Association of Women in EECS (AWE) at the Berkeley EECS Women’s History Month Kickoff Event! The event will include breakfast, and you can take home your very own succulent! Event Details: Wozniak Lounge, 9:30am – 11:45am on Friday, March 1st.
chunks.question: What are the details of the WHM Kickoff Event?

chunks.text: February 22, 2019: Reintroducing Joseph Thomas Gier. Joseph T. Gier was a pioneer in every sense. He was the first Black tenured faculty member in the UC system, a successful inventor, and a world authority on infrared measurement. Who was Berkeley EECS Prof. Joseph Gier?
chunks.question: Who was Joseph T. Gier?
chunks.answer: First Black tenured UC faculty, inventor, infrared expert.

chunks.text: February 8, 2019: The 2019 EECS Distinguished Alumni. Berkeley EECS has established a Distinguished Alumni Award to recognize the valuable contributions of its most distinguished alumni. EE alumnus Sharad Malik (M.S. ’87/EE Ph.D ’90, advisor: Robert k. Brayton), currently chair of Electrical Engineering at Princeton, was recognized for excellence in teaching, and groundbreaking research on design…
chunks.question: Who received the 2019 EECS Distinguished Alumni Award?
chunks.answer: Sharad Malik, EE alumnus and Princeton chair.

chunks.text: July 26, 2018: Enter the BlackBerry Spark Hackathon. Enter the BlackBerry’s Spark Hackathon for the chance to win a free trip to New York or London, where they will demo their application at a BlackBerry Security Summit. Can you please pass this invitation along to your students who excel in computer science? It’s a really exciting opportunity for…
chunks.question: What is the BlackBerry Spark Hackathon?
chunks.answer: Hackathon with trip prize to demo at summit.

chunks.text: July 26, 2018: Video Game & Board Game Playtest! The Nuclear Science and Security Consortium (NSSC) and the Project on Nuclear Gaming (PoNG) invite you to playtest a game called Signal, developed in partnership between UC Berkeley students and scientists from both Sandia and Lawrence Livermore National Laboratories. Signal will be playtested in…
chunks.question: What is the Signal game playtest about?
chunks.answer: Game developed by UC Berkeley and national labs.

chunks.text: June 14, 2018: AISES: Great Scholarship Opportunity with Intel! Applications Closing May 15. I hope you can help me get the word out about this scholarship for Native students – both undergraduates and graduates. I am extending the scholarship deadline for the Intel scholarships to May 15, 2018. Thank you so much for your help in getting the word out about this scholarship. The…
chunks.question: What is the AISES scholarship opportunity?
chunks.answer: Intel scholarship for Native students, deadline May 15.

chunks.text: June 14, 2018: Who wants to be a Google Games Champion? Come on down, Berkeley students! Do you want to be a contestant at this years Google Games? Is your team ready to challenge yourselves, win prizes and have fun? Well, we invite you to register for The Google Games Bay Area taking place on…
chunks.question: What is the Google Games event?
chunks.answer: Contest for Berkeley students to win prizes.

chunks.text: June 13, 2018: Blockchain Funding Opportunities. WHO WE ARE mouseBelt Labs is an ICO startup accelerator funding 10 companies in 2018 through our classOne program starting August 1. We also fund academic blockchain projects through our summerOne grant program starting June 15. WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR: We want to support students, grad students and…
chunks.question: What does mouseBelt Labs offer?
chunks.answer: Funding for blockchain startups and academic projects.

chunks.text: June 1, 2018: uLab Machine Learning Job Positions. Interested in deep learning? Have you wanted to apply computational concepts to other fields such as medicine or art? Have you ever wanted to get experience running a research group? The Statistical Modeling and Deep Learning uLab is looking for machine learning staff scientists, project leaders,…
chunks.question: What job positions are available at uLab?
chunks.answer: Machine learning staff scientists and project leaders.
